articles.title: Weekend need-to-know: Bloordale goes car-free
articles.summary: Big on Bloor is back to shut down a stretch of Bloor Street this weekend. Here’s what’s happening this weekend in Toronto. Big on Bloor 2026 Bloor is going car-free. Or at least a portion of it is this weekend for the annual Big on Bloor Festival. Celebrating all the small business that make up […]

articles.title: Female youth allegedly choked and assaulted in Vaughan abandoned building; male suspect wanted
articles.summary: Police say the alleged attack happened on Sunday, July 12, after officers were called to a derelict property near Jane Street and Hwy. 7.

articles.title: Gunfire damages restaurant in Etobicoke plaza; Toronto police investigating
articles.summary: Toronto police are investigating an early‑morning firearm discharge in Etobicoke after officers found damage at a restaurant in a commercial plaza. Police were called around 7:55 a.m. on Thursday to the La Rose Avenue and Royal York Road area near Eglinton Avenue West for what was initially reported as a suspicious incident. When officers arrived, […]

articles.title: 2 ejected, 1 critically injured in overnight Whitchurch‑Stouffville crash
articles.summary: York Regional Police (YRP) say a man and a woman were injured, one of them critically, following a single‑vehicle collision in Whitchurch‑Stouffville early Thursday. Investigators say the crash happened around 12:30 a.m. in the area of Bloomington Road and Kennedy Road, where a Mercedes SUV left the roadway. The impact caused both occupants to be […]

articles.title: Markham is hosting an IndyCar race next month. Is the city ready?
articles.summary: The Ontario Honda Dealers Indy at Markham will take place in the city from Friday, Aug. 14 to Sunday, Aug. 16, and the city says it’s ready to welcome the drivers, along with tens of thousands of race fans.

articles.title: 2 dead, 3 injured in Highway 401 crash in Mississauga, OPP says
articles.summary: Ontario Provincial Police say two people are dead and three injured after a crash on Highway 401 in Mississauga.

articles.title: FIFA Fan Festival, Nathan Phillips Square watch parties cancelled due to poor air quality
articles.summary: Several World Cup watch parties in Toronto, including FIFA Fan Festival, have been cancelled due to an orange-level air quality warning.

articles.title: Hamilton man facing 136 charges after children rescued from online exploitation in Philippines: Toronto police
articles.summary: An international investigation has led to the arrest of a 42-year-old Canadian man who allegedly paid for and directed the livestream sexual abuse of children overseas. Police say another man in the Philippines was also arrested, and four children were rescued from abuse by investigators.

articles.title: Teen who fatally stabbed elderly woman in Pickering receives 10-year maximum youth sentence
articles.summary: A 15-year-old boy who pleaded guilty to murdering an elderly woman in Pickering last year has received a 10-year sentence — the maximum for a youth convicted of first-degree murder.

articles.title: Gun licence applications have surged in the Toronto area since 2016
articles.summary: The number of people in the Greater Toronto and Hamilton area applying for their firearms licences has increased significantly in the last decade, most notably in Peel region. It’s not clear what exactly is behind the increase, though it appears — at least in part — to be linked to growing interest in shooting sports.
